scope_boundary.capability: Commit a proposed block to the canonical chain
scope_boundary.notes: the core outcome — proposed blocks become canonical

scope_boundary.capability: Store the canonical chain ledger and track its head
Bootstrap the genesis chain and initialise the supply
scope_boundary.notes: one-time, before the consensus loop runs
not this release; committed history is immutable

invariants.invariant: A committed block is immutable — it never changes or disappears.
invariants.business_reason: Committed history must be a permanent, reliable record other subdomains can trust.

invariants.invariant: Every committed block has exactly one predecessor, except the genesis block.
invariants.business_reason: The chain is a single, ordered history with one origin.

invariants.invariant: A block cannot be committed twice.
invariants.business_reason: Prevents duplication or replay of committed history.

invariants.invariant: Exactly one genesis block exists per chain, and genesis executes exactly once.
invariants.business_reason: The chain has a single, permanent origin and initial state.

invariants.invariant: Total supply is conserved at 1,000,000 BachiCoin after genesis.
invariants.business_reason: The monetary system is closed — no supply enters or leaves except by the system's rules.

invariants.invariant: A proposed block is not authoritative until committed.
invariants.business_reason: The chain is the sole authority for committed history.

invariants.invariant: Wallet balances are derived from committed transactions, not owned by the chain as independent state.
invariants.business_reason: Balances must always be consistent with the canonical committed history.

actions.trigger: the consensus loop produces a proposed block
actions.source_finding: S4 relationships Chain commits Proposed Block

actions.trigger: system initialisation, once, before the consensus loop runs
actions.source_finding: S4 relationships Bootstrap creates Genesis Block

provisional_codes.summary: Admit a request to commit a proposed block to the canonical chain.
Commit a proposed block: validate its predecessor link, sign it, record it as canonical, and reconcile balances.
Admit a request to bootstrap the genesis chain and initialise the supply.
Bootstrap the genesis chain: create the genesis block and initialise the fixed supply before consensus runs.
Record a validated, signed proposed block as canonical on the chain and announce it committed.
Check that a proposed block's predecessor link matches the current chain head before commit.
Create the genesis block containing the initial mint and record it as the first committed block.
Reconcile wallet balances from the committed transactions after a block commits.
